title: Maximum Sum of Subarray with K Consecutive Elements

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers `nums` and an integer `k`, find the maximum sum of a subarray that has at least `k` consecutive elements.
statement.inputFormat: Input is an array of integers and an integer `k`.
statement.outputFormat: The maximum sum of a subarray with at least `k` consecutive elements.
statement.notes: Consecutive elements have the same index difference.

examples.explanation: Maximum sum is 2+4+6+7+9+3+5 = 36, but the subarray 2+4 has less than k=3 elements, so we consider (2+4)+(4+6)+(6+7)+(7+9)+(9+3)+(3+5) = 36. Then we find another subarray with 3 elements: (7+9+3) and its adjacent subarray with 3 elements: (9+3+5) with the max sum 31.

hints.text: First, find the maximum sum of a subarray with k consecutive elements. Then, consider all subarrays with k consecutive elements that are adjacent to the previous subarray with k elements.

editorial.title: Maximum Sum of Subarray with K Consecutive Elements
editorial.intuition: The idea is to find the maximum sum of a subarray with k consecutive elements and consider all subarrays with k consecutive elements that are adjacent to the previous subarray with k elements.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach: Generate all possible subarrays with k consecutive elements and find the maximum sum.
editorial.betterSolution: Greedy approach: Maintain a window of size k and always try to expand the window to the right to get the maximum sum.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution: Maintain a window of size k and always try to expand the window to the right to get the maximum sum. This can be implemented using a prefix sum array.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The greedy approach is correct because we always try to expand the window to the right to get the maximum sum.
editorial.dryRun: 1. Initialize a prefix sum array with the first k elements of the array.

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution {public: int maxSumOfSubarrayWithKConsecutiveElements(vector<int>& nums, int k) { // TODO: implement int sum = 0; for (int i = 0; i < k; i++) sum += nums[i]; int maxSum = sum; for (int start = 0; start <= nums.size() - k; start++) { int currentSum = sum; for (int end = start + 1; end < start + k; end++) currentSum += nums[end]; maxSum = std::max(maxSum, currentSum); } return sum; }};
